subject: [PATCH v6 00/15] userfaultfd: working set tracking for VM guest memory

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport (Microsoft)
reviews.summary: Approved the addition of VM_UFFD_RWP to VM_COPY_ON_FORK and the preservation of RWP markers across PTE rewrites. However, suggested that the patch series should be rebased on top of a more recent version of the userfaultfd/pagemap: pre-existing fixes series.

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (Meta)
reviews.summary: Responded to Mike's comments, stating that the patch series will be rebased on top of a more recent version of the userfaultfd/pagemap: pre-existing fixes series. Also mentioned that the addition of VM_UFFD_RWP to VM_COPY_ON_FORK and the preservation of RWP markers across PTE rewrites are essential for the correct functioning of the new registration mode.

2026-05-29.patch_summary: This patch series adds userfaultfd support for tracking the working set of VM guest memory, enabling a VMM to identify cold pages and evict them to tiered or remote storage. The approach introduces a new userfaultfd registration mode (MODE_RWP) that uses PAGE_NONE on PTEs to mark pages as inaccessible while keeping them resident. This allows the kernel to deliver UFFD_PAGEFAULT_FLAG_RWP messages to registered handlers, which can resolve faults with UFFDIO_RWPROTECT.
